status: internal premium commercial package draft, not buyer-ready final copy
boundary_statement: This package describes a pre-deployment diagnostic and architecture-stage review engagement. It is not a benchmark, certification, compliance audit, security assessment, deployment approval, runtime monitor, or guarantee of AI safety.
premium_positioning: SpecShift Architectural Logic Diagnostics helps organizations evaluate high-consequence AI-agent workflows before scale by stress-testing where automated reasoning, evidence handling, scope boundaries, handoffs, escalation logic, and recovery paths may fail under synthetic edge-case review.
executive_thesis: Enterprise AI-agent risk is no longer only about whether a model can answer correctly. The higher-value risk is whether an agentic workflow acts on weak evidence, stale state, unclear authority, or false completion while sounding operationally confident. SpecShift provides a bounded way to expose those failure patterns before they become expensive integration, governance, customer, or trust problems.
why_now: AI-agent workflows are moving from demos into operational decision paths.
Standard evaluations often test output quality, not workflow integrity.
Multi-system agents can fail through stale data, permission overreach, unclear handoffs, and false final-state claims.
Teams need architecture-stage diagnostics before broad rollout, not after incidents.

decision_maker_pain_framing.ceo_or_founder: Avoid scaling an agent workflow that later damages customer trust or enterprise credibility.
decision_maker_pain_framing.cto_or_vp_engineering: Find logic, handoff, state, and evidence-handling failures before integration debt compounds.
decision_maker_pain_framing.chief_product_or_ai_officer: Define where the workflow is ready for more testing and where the user experience needs guardrails.
decision_maker_pain_framing.risk_legal_or_governance_lead: Create a structured internal map of known limitations without pretending it is certification or compliance sign-off.
decision_maker_pain_framing.enterprise_buyer: Understand whether a proposed AI-agent workflow has clear review thresholds before committing to broader deployment.

strategic_offer.name: SpecShift Strategic Workflow Evaluation
strategic_offer.scope: One high-value AI-agent workflow, reviewed through adversarial stress tests, limitation mapping, workflow logic review, and escalation-path analysis.
strategic_offer.duration: Typically 4 to 6 weeks depending on workflow complexity and access to materials.
inputs_needed: Representative prompts, tasks, or decision paths
Known approval, handoff, and escalation points
Existing test results, incident examples, or concern areas if available

premium_deliverables_menu.buyer_value: Gives leadership a concise view of where the target workflow appears stable under synthetic review and where risk concentrates.
Shows how the workflow responds to unverified inputs, stale state, ambiguous authority, false completion, handoff gaps, and escalation pressure.
Documents where automated logic should stop, slow down, ask for confirmation, or route to human review.
Identifies scope overextension, multi-system inconsistency, stale data assumptions, and unclear ownership transfer.
Turns vague oversight into concrete review triggers and recovery pathways.
Converts findings into prioritized design-review actions without claiming certification or deployment approval.

pilot_tiers.fit: One high-value workflow, bounded access, executive memo plus core diagnostic outputs.
pilot_tiers.boundary: No exclusivity, no production certification, no code transfer, no training rights.

pilot_tiers.fit: Deeper workflow evaluation, multiple scenario families, expanded leadership readout, and design-review roadmap.
pilot_tiers.boundary: Still diagnostic and architecture-stage; no compliance sign-off or safety guarantee.

pilot_tiers.fit: Time-bounded, narrow use rights for internal application of selected review structures to approved workflows.
pilot_tiers.boundary: Non-exclusive by default unless specifically priced and constrained; no broad method transfer without separate agreement.

why_this_is_not_ordinary_consulting: The method is built around adversarial failure discovery, not generic best-practice commentary.
Each review asks where the workflow breaks, not only where it performs well.
The artifact stack includes schema controls, local validation, claim-boundary discipline, and structured failure columns.
The output is designed to help teams make design decisions, not decorate a slide deck.
safe_claims: SpecShift provides a structured internal review of AI-agent workflow logic under synthetic stress conditions.
SpecShift can identify candidate failure modes involving evidence handling, scope boundaries, stale state, handoff gaps, escalation thresholds, and recovery paths.
SpecShift outputs can support architecture-stage design decisions and paid pilot conversations.
forbidden_claims: Do not claim runtime monitoring or runtime protection.
Do not claim guaranteed safety, security, or reliability.
Do not claim validated performance without external evidence.
qualification_gate: Buyer has a concrete AI-agent workflow or near-term deployment path.
Buyer has meaningful downside if the workflow fails silently.
Buyer can provide enough workflow context for synthetic stress review.
Buyer accepts that the engagement is diagnostic, not certification.
Buyer is prepared for a paid pilot rather than free exploratory consulting.
disqualification_gate: Buyer wants certification or compliance sign-off.
Buyer wants broad IP transfer or training rights.
Buyer wants to test vague AI strategy without a concrete workflow.
Buyer cannot accept strict claim boundaries.
suggested_call_to_action: Select one high-value AI-agent workflow and run a bounded SpecShift diagnostic pilot to map where its automated logic, evidence handling, handoffs, review thresholds, and recovery paths may fail under synthetic stress conditions.
failure_column: This strategic package stops fitting if it is used to imply certification, validated performance, compliance sign-off, runtime protection, broad safety claims, or production approval. It also stops fitting if sold to buyers without a concrete workflow, meaningful downside, and willingness to pay for a bounded diagnostic engagement.
next_internal_step: Use this package to create an outreach-safe email and a one-page executive PDF only after a final claim-boundary review.
